```text
## Accumulated Context

### Decisions

Decisions are logged in PROJECT.md Key Decisions table.
Recent decisions affecting current work:

- Roadmap: 7 phases following strict dependency chain (pipeline -> data model -> import -> frontend -> content)
- Research flagged Phase 3 (import) as highest-risk operation -- test batch sizes on actual host
- [01-02] Removed sys.exit(1) on zero-event runs: warnings are informational, not fatal -- pipeline must always produce master_events.csv
- [01-02] Added last_run_counts.json so operators can distinguish new regression from known recurring 0-event state
- [01-02] run_health_check returns 3-tuple (summary, any_zero, current_counts) -- caller (main) holds persistence responsibility
- [Phase 01-pipeline-stabilization]: Dal Art Gallery selectors confirmed working; 0 events is dark period not selector bug (all 10 listed events are past as of 2026-03-11)
- [Phase 01-pipeline-stabilization]: hfxcomedyfest: Tixr uses DataDome (not Cloudflare); Playwright headless blocked; fix requires playwright-stealth or residential proxy; left disabled
- [Phase 02-01]: VENUE_ADDRESSES uses Bearly's relocated address (1579 Grafton, not 1269 Barrington) per official website
- [Phase 02-01]: VENUE_ADDRESSES uses Yuk Yuk's current address (1665 Argyle, not 1181 Hollis) per Waze and Downtown Halifax Business Commission
- [Phase 02-01]: generate_venue_csv.py accepts output_path param for testability; defaults to output/venue_import.csv
- [Phase 02-03]: Active theme is Twenty Twenty-Five (no child theme) -- custom PHP location: wp-content/mu-plugins/
- [Phase 02-03]: neighbourhood taxonomy registered as hfxnow_nbhd_tax_v2 (renamed due to WPCode name conflict); rewrite:false to avoid TEC conflicts
- [Phase 02-03]: mu-plugin is canonical location for all custom WordPress PHP going forward
- [Phase 02-02]: tribe_events_cat requires exactly 17 canonical terms; WP-CLI batch create is idempotent (errors on existing names but does not duplicate)
- [Phase 02-02]: TEC venue import uses admin UI (Events > Import > Venues file type) — no WP-CLI equivalent for the importer
- [Phase 03-event-import-and-venue-tagging]: [03-02] User chose Option C: skip WP deletion of ~20 Phase 1 test events; near-duplicates to be addressed post-import if needed
- [Phase 03-event-import-and-venue-tagging]: [03-02] For archive-reset bulk-import prep: run merge_master_events.py standalone, not via master_runner.py
- [Phase 03-event-import-and-venue-tagging]: Test batch import passed: no category normalization errors, no mass TEC import failures — safe to proceed to Plan 03-04 bulk import
- [Phase 03-event-import-and-venue-tagging]: [03-03] ~20 Phase 1 test events did not cause issues during test batch import — deferred near-duplicate review to post-bulk-import
- [Phase 03-event-import-and-venue-tagging]: Bulk import of 400 events succeeded in a single batch — no need to split into contingency batches; Plesk host handled 400-event CSV without timeout

### Pending Todos

None yet.

- Hosting environment specs unknown -- affects import batch sizing (validate before Phase 3)
- Happy hour venue count unconfirmed -- Phase 7 requires 15+ venues minimum
```

```text
# Halifax Now

## What This Is

Halifax Now (www.halifax-now.ca) is a comprehensive local events and social planning website for Halifax, NS. It aggregates events from across the city — including hard-to-find sources like Instagram — and presents them in one place. Built on WordPress with Events Calendar Pro and TEC Filter Bar, it serves locals and visitors who want to plan something social, whether tonight or two months out.

## Core Value

The only Halifax events site that covers everything — if something's happening in Halifax, it's here.

## Requirements

### Validated

(None yet — ship to validate)

### Active

- [ ] Full events calendar working cleanly (Events Calendar Pro + TEC Filter Bar)
- [ ] Neighbourhood guides section
- [ ] Happy hour / food & drink specials section
- [ ] Scraper pipeline produces clean, importable CSVs
- [ ] Site is visually organized and easy to navigate

### Out of Scope

- Automated CSV import — manual upload is fine for v1; automate later
- Mobile app — web-first
- User-submitted events — curated only for v1
- Venues/restaurants directory — v2
- Recurring weekly events (run clubs, open mics, etc.) — v2
- Real-time event data — scraper cadence is sufficient

## Context

- WordPress site is already live at www.halifax-now.ca/events but described as "a bit of a mess" — needs cleanup and proper organization
- Existing scraper pipeline: individual site scrapers → merge script → CSV → manual import to WordPress
- Scrapers target local Halifax event websites plus Instagram and other hard-to-find sources
- Events Calendar Pro and TEC Filter Bar are the core plugins for the calendar and filtering UX
- Builder has deep personal familiarity with the Halifax event landscape — this started as a personal pain point
- Future content expansion planned: venues/restaurants, recurring weekly programming, food & drink specials, recreation/run clubs

## Constraints

- **Tech Stack**: WordPress with Events Calendar Pro + TEC Filter Bar — not changing
- **Data pipeline**: Python scrapers producing CSV for manual import — automation is v2
- **Scope**: Halifax, NS only for v1

## Key Decisions

| Decision | Rationale | Outcome |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| WordPress + Events Calendar Pro | Already in use, has required functionality | — Pending |
| Manual CSV import | Lower complexity for v1, automate later | — Pending |
| Curated only (no user submissions) | Quality control, reduce moderation overhead | — Pending |

---
*Last updated: 2026-03-11 after initialization*
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
---
phase: 04-calendar-and-filtered-views

<current_state>
Executing Phase 4 (Calendar and Filtered Views). Plan 04-01 is complete with SUMMARY. Plan 04-02 is in progress — both WordPress pages (/tonight and /this-weekend) have been created, and Task 3 (human verification checkpoint) is pending. The updated hfxnow-calendar-ui.php has NOT yet been confirmed uploaded or verified on the server.
</current_state>

<completed_work>

**Plan 04-01 — COMPLETE (04-01-SUMMARY.md exists)**
- Task 1 (human): TEC display settings — list view default, 25 events/page ✓
- Task 2 (auto): Created wp-content/mu-plugins/hfxnow-calendar-ui.php ✓
- Task 3 (human): Filter Bar configured with 4 filters (Category, Date Range, Neighbourhood, Venue) ✓
- Free Only filter dropped — TEC Filter Bar 5.6.x V2 incompatibility with mu-plugin custom filters
- Hook later fixed: switched from `tribe_template_after_include:events/v2/list/event/venue` to `tribe_template_after_include:events/v2/list/event/title` + uses context for event ID (commit 679f368)
- Cost injection removed (TEC v2 natively displays cost)

**Plan 04-02 — IN PROGRESS (no SUMMARY yet)**
- Task 1 (human): /tonight page created and published ✓
- Task 2 (human): /this-weekend page created and published ✓
- Task 3 (human-verify): NOT YET COMPLETE — user has not typed "approved"

**WordPress pages created via WP-CLI** (Gutenberg REST API was broken due to Nginx "upstream sent too big header" error — fastcgi_buffer_size directives couldn't be applied in Plesk)
</completed_work>

<remaining_work>

1. **User uploads updated hfxnow-calendar-ui.php** (commit 679f368) to wp-content/mu-plugins/ on server
   - The fix: category injection now uses title hook + context event ID
   - Upload via SFTP or Plesk file manager (same location as before)

2. **Verify category appears on event cards** at /events/ after upload
   - If still not showing → events may have no tribe_events_cat terms assigned from import (data issue)

3. **Investigate filter bar not populating**
   - Filter Bar appears but filter dropdowns show no options
   - Most likely: events were imported without category/neighbourhood taxonomy terms
   - Check: WP Admin > Events > open any event — does it have categories in the sidebar?
   - If no categories: need to batch-assign taxonomy terms (separate task, not in current plan)

4. **Task 3 human-verify checkpoint** — user types "approved" confirming CAL requirements met
   - CAL-01: cost+category on cards (pending category fix)
   - CAL-02: 4-filter bar (Free Only dropped, 4 filters is the deliverable)
   - CAL-03: /tonight works ✓
   - CAL-04: /this-weekend works ✓
   - CAL-05: Free Only — NOT delivered (dropped)
   - CAL-06: Neighbourhood filter (may be empty if no events have neighbourhood terms)

5. **Create 04-02-SUMMARY.md** after verification approval
6. **Update STATE.md and ROADMAP.md** via gsd-tools
7. **Run verifier agent** for Phase 4
8. **Mark Phase 4 complete** if verification passes (or plan gap closure if gaps found)
</remaining_work>

<decisions_made>

- Free Only filter dropped entirely — TEC Filter Bar 5.6.x V2 views require Context_Filter trait + context-to-filter map registration that can't be done from a mu-plugin cleanly. Not worth complexity for low-priority feature.
- Filter Bar delivered with 4 filters instead of planned 5. CAL-02 and CAL-05 marked as not fully met.
- WordPress pages created via WP-CLI because Gutenberg REST API blocked by Nginx buffer size issue on Plesk host.
- Cost injection removed from mu-plugin — TEC v2 natively shows cost on list view cards, our injection was redundant.
- Category hook changed from venue template hook to title template hook — venue template skipped when events have no venue linked.
</decisions_made>

<blockers>

- **Nginx buffer size**: Gutenberg editor publishing fails with "upstream sent too big header". Plesk Additional nginx directives field rejected fastcgi_buffer_size as "invalid header". Workaround: use WP-CLI or Classic Editor for any future page publishing. Not blocking current work.
- **Filter bar empty options**: Filters appear but show no options — likely events have no taxonomy terms assigned from Phase 3 import. Needs investigation after category fix is confirmed.
</blockers>

<context>
Phase 4 is nearly done. The two new pages work. The main outstanding question is whether event cards show category (needs updated mu-plugin upload) and whether the filter bar can populate (data issue — may need batch taxonomy assignment). Once category is confirmed working and user approves the verification checkpoint, we create 04-02-SUMMARY.md and run the verifier agent to close out Phase 4.

The mu-plugin at wp-content/mu-plugins/hfxnow-calendar-ui.php is the only file changed in this project. The WordPress site itself is the main thing being verified.
</context>

<next_action>
Start with: Ask user if they have uploaded updated wp-content/mu-plugins/hfxnow-calendar-ui.php (commit 679f368) to server. Then check /events/ for category text on cards. Then investigate whether events have tribe_events_cat terms assigned (WP Admin > Events > any event > sidebar categories).
</next_action>
```

```text
      via: "Shortcode block in WP block editor"
      pattern: "tribe_events shortcode"
    - from: "/this-weekend WordPress page"
      to: "hfxnow_this_weekend shortcode"
      via: "Shortcode block in WP block editor — wrapper dynamically computes Fri/Sun dates"
      pattern: "hfxnow_this_weekend"
    - from: "Filter Bar on shortcode pages"
      to: "tribe-bar=true shortcode attribute"
      via: "TEC Filter Bar Pro shortcode support (TEC 6.x)"
      pattern: "tribe-bar"
---

<objective>
Create the "Tonight in Halifax" and "This Weekend in Halifax" WordPress pages using TEC shortcodes, then run the final human-verify checkpoint confirming all 6 CAL requirements are met across all three calendar pages.

Purpose: Completes the filterable calendar experience with dedicated date-specific views so users can quickly find what's happening today or this weekend.
Output: Two live WordPress pages (/tonight, /this-weekend) each showing a date-filtered event list with full Filter Bar. Human sign-off that Phase 4 success criteria are met.
</objective>

Shortcodes now available in WordPress:
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-16 13:50:44Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Find and read the mu-plugin file

`cat "/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/wp-content/mu-plugins/hfxnow-calendar-ui.php" 2>/dev/null || find "/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3" -name "hfxnow-calendar-ui.php" 2>/dev/null`

```text
<?php
/**
 * HFXNow Calendar UI Customizations
 *
 * - Adds cost and category to list view event cards
 * - Registers [hfxnow_this_weekend] shortcode for This Weekend page
 *
 * Location: wp-content/mu-plugins/hfxnow-calendar-ui.php
 */
if ( ! defined( 'ABSPATH' ) ) {
    exit;
}

// =============================================================================
// SECTION 1 — Cost + Category on list view cards
// =============================================================================

/**
 * Inject primary category below the title on list view event cards.
 *
 * Uses the title hook (not venue) because the venue template is skipped
 * when events have no venue linked, making it unreliable.
 * TEC v2 natively displays cost, so we only inject category here.
 *
 * Hook: tribe_template_after_include:events/v2/list/event/title
 * Priority: 10, Args: 3
 */
add_action(
    'tribe_template_after_include:events/v2/list/event/title',
    function ( $file, $name, $context ) {
        // In TEC v2, prefer event from template context; fall back to get_the_ID().
        $event    = $context->get( 'event' );
        $event_id = ( $event instanceof WP_Post ) ? $event->ID : get_the_ID();

        // Primary category — first term from tribe_events_cat taxonomy.
        $cats = get_the_terms( $event_id, 'tribe_events_cat' );
        if ( $cats && ! is_wp_error( $cats ) ) {
            echo '<div class="hfxnow-event-category">' . esc_html( $cats[0]->name ) . '</div>';
        }
    },
    10,
    3
);

// =============================================================================
// SECTION 2 — [hfxnow_this_weekend] shortcode
// =============================================================================

/**
 * Shortcode: [hfxnow_this_weekend]
 *
 * Renders a TEC list view filtered to the Friday–Sunday window for the
 * current week. Works correctly when viewed Mon–Thu (upcoming weekend),
 * Friday (starts today), Saturday or Sunday (current weekend).
 *
 * @return string  Output of the embedded [tribe_events] shortcode.
 */
add_shortcode(
    'hfxnow_this_weekend',
    function () {
        $dow = (int) date( 'N' ); // 1 = Monday … 5 = Friday, 6 = Saturday, 7 = Sunday

        if ( $dow === 5 ) {
            // Today is Friday — weekend starts now.
            $friday = date( 'Y-m-d' );
            $sunday = date( 'Y-m-d', strtotime( 'this sunday' ) );
        } elseif ( $dow >= 6 ) {
            // Today is Saturday or Sunday — weekend already started.
            $friday = date( 'Y-m-d', strtotime( 'last friday' ) );
            $sunday = date( 'Y-m-d', strtotime( 'this sunday' ) );
        } else {
            // Mon–Thu — show the upcoming weekend.
            $friday = date( 'Y-m-d', strtotime( 'this friday' ) );
            $sunday = date( 'Y-m-d', strtotime( 'this sunday' ) );
        }

        $sc = sprintf(
            '[tribe_events view="list" start_date="%s" end_date="%s" tribe-bar="true" events_per_page="100"]',
            $friday,
            $sunday
        );

        return do_shortcode( $sc );
    }
);
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-16 13:51:06Z)**_

The mu-plugin code looks fine. Let me think about what's happening:

1. **503 nginx bad gateway** - This is a server issue. Could be PHP-FPM running out of workers, memory exhaustion, etc. The mu-plugin itself looks syntactically fine. But could the TEC shortcode being rendered on pages be causing timeouts?

2. **Neighbourhood taxonomy disappeared** - The neighbourhood taxonomy (`hfxnow_nbhd_tax_v2`) was registered in a previous version of this mu-plugin. But looking at the current file, I DON'T SEE the neighbourhood taxonomy registration! The file only has:
   - Category injection on event cards
   - The `[hfxnow_this_weekend]` shortcode

There's no `register_taxonomy()` call for `hfxnow_nbhd_tax_v2` at all. That's why the neighbourhood taxonomy disappeared—the updated mu-plugin must have removed that registration code.

3. **Categories not showing on cards** - The code looks correct—it hooks after the title template and injects `tribe_events_cat` terms. But the user says categories aren't showing, which could mean events don't have categories assigned.

The user confirmed that events don't have `tribe_events_cat` terms assigned from the import, so this is a data issue rather than a display problem. They also mentioned the card layout might not have enough space for categories anyway, so even if the data were there, the design would need adjustment.

Looking at the bigger picture, I need to tackle three things in order: first, the 503 nginx errors which are blocking everything—likely a PHP-FPM exhaustion issue that I can help diagnose through Plesk logs; second, restore the missing `hfxnow_nbhd_tax_v2` taxonomy registration in the mu-plugin since it was removed in the latest version; and third, handle the category assignment problem once the server is stable. The user mentioned there isn't enough space on the cards for categories, which is a design constraint I need to factor in. Before I respond to them, I should review the previous plan documentation to understand what the neighbourhood taxonomy setup was and what changed.
</details></think>

```text
# Plan 04-01: TEC Display Settings + Filter Bar + Calendar UI Plugin Summary

**TEC configured for list view (25/page), Filter Bar with 4 filters, cost+category injected on event cards via mu-plugin**

## Performance

- **Duration:** ~60 min (includes extended Free Only filter debugging)
- **Completed:** 2026-03-13
- **Tasks:** 3 (Tasks 1 and 3 human-action; Task 2 auto)
- **Files modified:** 1

## Accomplishments

- TEC display settings: /events/ now defaults to list view, 25 events per page
- Filter Bar live with Category, Date Range, Neighbourhood, Venue filters in order
- Event cards in list view show cost text and primary category as plain text
- [hfxnow_this_weekend] shortcode registered and ready for Plan 04-02

## Task Commits

1. **Task 2: Create hfxnow-calendar-ui.php mu-plugin** — `b32359f` (feat)
2. **Fix: Remove unworkable Free Only filter** — `7f8dbd7` (fix)

## Files Created/Modified

- `wp-content/mu-plugins/hfxnow-calendar-ui.php` — cost/category card injection, hfxnow_this_weekend shortcode

## Decisions Made

Free Only filter dropped. TEC Filter Bar 5.6.x V2 views require custom filters to use the `Context_Filter` trait and be registered via `tribe_events_filter_bar_context_to_filter_map` — both need deep integration with TEC's service container that isn't achievable cleanly from a mu-plugin. Multiple hook approaches failed. Feature is low-priority and not worth the complexity.

## Deviations from Plan

**Free Only filter not delivered.** All other deliverables complete. CAL-02 (5-filter bar) and CAL-05 (Free Only functional) are not fully met — Filter Bar has 4 filters instead of 5.

## Issues Encountered

TEC Filter Bar 5.6.x V2 views don't expose a reliable hook for third-party custom filters from mu-plugins. The old `tribe_events_filters_create_filters` hook works for admin registration but not for V2 frontend rendering, which requires the `Context_Filter` trait and a context-to-filter map registration that ties into TEC internals. Dropped after extended debugging.

## Next Phase Readiness

- /events/ list view, 25/page, 4-filter Filter Bar, cost+category cards: all ready ✓
- [hfxnow_this_weekend] shortcode ready for Plan 04-02 ✓
- Free Only filter: not delivered — can be revisited as a standalone plugin if needed post-launch

---
*Phase: 04-calendar-and-filtered-views*
*Completed: 2026-03-13*
```
</details></tool-use>
